food_name: Starbucks VIA Caffè Mocha Instant Coffee
aliases: Starbucks VIA Ready Brew Caffè Mocha

english: Starbucks VIA Ready Brew Caffè Mocha

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general instant coffee beverage class; no specific mocha-flavoured instant coffee with chocolate exists. FOODON_03301116 (instant coffee beverage) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EKP covers instant coffee generically; no mocha-specific or chocolate-flavoured instant coffee sub-code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34658001 is the closest defensible substance code for coffee. No specific instant mocha coffee product code exists in SNOMED CT.

allergens: milk (non-dairy creamer may contain milk derivatives)

serving.description: 1 stick (approximately 22 g) dissolved in hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one stick packet (approximately 22 g) dissolved in approximately 237 ml of hot water, yielding one cup of mocha-flavoured instant coffee.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks VIA Caffè Mocha — Nutrition Facts (USDA Branded Food / Starbucks published label data)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks VIA Caffè Mocha branded product nutrition data sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods database, which ingests manufacturer-submitted label data. Per-100 g values calculated from the per-stick (22 g) label values: Energy 80 kcal, Protein 1 g, Total Fat 1.5 g, Saturated Fat 1 g, Total Carbohydrate 17 g, Total Sugars 14 g, Dietary Fibre 1 g, Sodium 95 mg per stick. Divided by 0.22 to obtain per-100 g figures.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sugar-containing instant coffee beverage mix — analogous estimate based on sucrose and cocoa-containing beverage data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks VIA Caffè Mocha in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous sugar-containing instant coffee and cocoa beverage mixes. The dominant glycemic driver is sucrose (GI ~65) and the cocoa/coffee matrix moderates the glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (55 × 15.4) / 100 ≈ 8.5. Available carbohydrate per 22 g serving = 77.3 × 0.22 × (1 − fibre fraction) ≈ 15.4 g net carbohydrate.

hypertension.guidance: Each stick provides approximately 95 mg sodium; individuals with hypertension should account for this within their daily sodium budget of under 2000 mg, and note that caffeine in the product may transiently elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and low glycemic index with moderate glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 22 g stick contains approximately 14 g of added sugars with a glycemic load of approximately 8.5. While the GI is low (55), the high sugar content and moderate glycemic load per serving make this product unsuitable for frequent consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; a sugar-free or unsweetened coffee alternative is strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving contributes approximately 1 g saturated fat and 14 g sugar; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and prefer unsweetened coffee options to reduce saturated fat and free sugar intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Starbucks VIA Caffè Mocha is an ultra-processed instant coffee beverage mix sold in individual stick packets, typically packaged in sets of four sticks. Each stick (approximately 22 g) is dissolved in hot water to produce a mocha-flavoured coffee drink combining the bitterness of instant coffee with the sweetness of cocoa and sugar. The product is manufactured by Starbucks and is positioned as a convenient, portable premium instant coffee experience. The flavour profile is rich, chocolatey, and moderately sweet, with a smooth texture when properly dissolved. Nutritionally, the product is high in sugar (approximately 63.6 g per 100 g, or 14 g per stick), moderate in sodium (approximately 432 mg per 100 g, or 95 mg per stick), and provides modest amounts of protein (4.5 g per 100 g) and dietary fibre (4.5 g per 100 g) from cocoa solids. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low category) with a glycemic load of approximately 8.5 per serving, making it a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome due to its high sugar content. The product is not suitable for vegans due to potential milk derivatives in the non-dairy creamer component. In Singapore, this product is available via field survey from Starbucks outlets and is classified as a packaged beverage mix rather than a freshly prepared café drink. Clinically, the high free sugar content is the primary concern for both glycaemic control and cardiovascular health.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods database using manufacturer-submitted label data for Starbucks VIA Caffè Mocha, which is an exact product match. Per-100 g values are calculated by scaling from the per-stick (22 g) label values. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct measurement exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for this specific product. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Per-100 g nutrient values are calculated by dividing per-stick (22 g) label values by 0.22. Trans fat is 0.0 g as explicitly stated on the product label (confirmed zero per USDA Branded Foods label data). Cholesterol is 0.0 mg as explicitly stated on the product label (confirmed zero per USDA Branded Foods label data). monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported on the product label or in the USDA Branded Foods entry for this product. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported on the product label or in the USDA Branded Foods entry for this product. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source label data. water_g is -1 as this is a dry powder product and water content of the dry mix is not reported in the source label data. GI is an analogous estimate of 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for sugar-containing cocoa and coffee beverage mixes; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. Multilingual local names are not applicable for this product as it is a US-origin branded packaged product (Starbucks VIA) sold globally; no Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese local-language names exist for this product. The cuisine category is Coffee (not Singaporean cuisine), so the Singaporean multilingual naming requirement (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) does not apply. The product is sold in Singapore via field survey at Starbucks - One Fullerton at SGD 8.70 for a 4-stick pack (SGD 2.175 per stick). Halal status is not confirmed; Starbucks Singapore halal certification applies to beverages prepared in-store but packaged retail products require separate verification.

improvement_actions_taken: Recategorised food_group from Mixed to Beverages to accurately reflect the product type.
Calculated per-100 g nutrient values from per-stick (22 g) label data sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods.
Set glycemic_index to 55 (medium) as an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data; documented estimation basis in gi_source.
Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes with explicit source justification.
Confirmed trans_fat_g and cholesterol_mg as 0.0 with confirmed zero justification in notes.

Applied G6 multilingual compliance correctly: this is Coffee cuisine (US-origin branded product), not Singaporean cuisine, so Chinese/Malay/Tamil/Hindi are not mandatory; local_names fields set to null with explanation in notes.
Ensured aliases_by_language english entries are verbatim consistent with flat aliases list.
Set all three health_context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
Verified ontology codes are real and defensible with closest_parent match_type and gap notes.
Set natural_language_description to well over 200 characters with full clinical and nutritional context.

Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55, as per standard GI classification (<=55 is low GI). Updated gi_source.source_match_notes, health_context.type2_diabetes.key_factor, and llm_training.natural_language_description to reflect the 'low' GI category.
